titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Anselm Hollo Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 12, 2023 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Anselm Hollo Papers contains correspondence, subject files, writings, teaching files, personal and professional papers, artwork, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual media by or relating to Anselm Hollo. Notable correspondents include John Ashbery, Amiri Baraka (LeRoi Jones), Ted Berrigan, Allen Ginsberg, Robert Grenier, Langston Hughes, Gerrit Lansing, Ron Padgett, and John Taggart. Writings contain Hollo's translations of works by Ginsberg, Pentti Saarikoski, Dylan Thomas, and William Carlos Williams, as well as original poetry, prose, and works edited by Hollo. The collection also contains articles, poems, translations, and complete works of other writers, including Mary Abrams, Jane Wodening, and Laura Wright. Teaching files include syllabi, outlines, class notes, other course materials, and student works. Personal and professional papers contain conference materials, grant applications, awards, additional writings, travel logs, ephemera, notebooks, financial records, and photographs. Artwork consists of collages, some of which are collaborative projects between Hollo and his wife, Jane Dalrymple-Hollo, oil pastel drawings, and sketches. Printed materials contain annotated books, serial publications, brochures, pamphlets, and newspapers. Computer and audiovisual media consist of compact discs, videocassettes, audiocassettes, and film.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 34 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 35 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, and the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Correspondence and Subject Files, 1943-2011 and undated. II. Writings, circa 1943-2016 and undated. III. Writings of Others, 1965-2010 and undated. IV. Teaching Files, 1976-2009 and undated. V. Personal and Professional Papers, 1934-2013 and undated. VI. Artwork, 1950-2008 and undated. VII. Printed Materials, circa 1907-2013 and undated. VIII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1960-2011 and undated.

bioghist.head: Anselm Hollo (1934-2013)
bioghist.p: Anselm Hollo was a Finnish poet and translator who published over 40 works of poetry. His awards and recognition included a fellowship from the National Endowment of the Arts, grants from the Fund for Poetry, Finland's Distinguished Foreign Translator's Award, and the Gertrude Stein Award in Innovative American Poetry. Hollo taught creative writing and poetry at institutions including The State University of New York College at Buffalo, the University of Colorado at Boulder, and the Naropa Institute in Boulder, Colorado.

Born in Helsinki, Finland on April 12, 1934, Hollo moved to the United Kingdom in the 1960s. There, he worked at the Finnish section of the BBC World Service, where he gained recognition as a poet and translator. In 1967 he moved to Boulder, Colorado and married visual artist Jane Dalrymple-Hollo. Hollo translated poetry and essays from Finnish, German, Swedish, and French into English, including Pentti Saarikoski's Trilogy (2003), for which he received the Harold Morton Landon Translation Award from the Academy of American Poets in 2004. He was also one of the early translators of Allen Ginsberg's poetry into German and Finnish. Hollo's poetry collections include Notes on the Possibilities and Attractions of Existence: New and Selected Poems, 1965-2000 (2001), which received the San Francisco Poetry Center Book Award. Anselm Hollo died on January 29, 2013 at the age of 78.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Anselm Hollo Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Anselm Hollo Papers contains correspondence, subject files, writings, teaching files, personal and professional papers, artwork, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual media by or relating to Anselm Hollo. Notable correspondents include John Ashbery, Amiri Baraka (LeRoi Jones), Ted Berrigan, Allen Ginsberg, Robert Grenier, Langston Hughes, Gerrit Lansing, Ron Padgett, and John Taggart. Writings contain Hollo's translations of works by Ginsberg, Pentti Saarikoski, Dylan Thomas, and William Carlos Williams, as well as original poetry, prose, and works edited by Hollo. The collection also contains articles, poems, translations, and complete works of other writers, including Mary Abrams, Jane Wodening, and Laura Wright. Teaching files include syllabi, outlines, class notes, other course materials, and student works. Personal and professional papers contain conference materials, grant applications, awards, additional writings, travel logs, ephemera, notebooks, financial records, and photographs. Artwork consists of oil pastel drawings, sketches, and collages, some of which are collaborative projects between Hollo and his wife, Jane Dalrymple-Hollo. Printed materials contain annotated books, serial publications, brochures, pamphlets, and newspapers. Computer and audiovisual media consist of compact discs, videocassettes, audiocassettes, and film.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Anselm Hollo Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing, indicated by brackets.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Correspondence and Subject Files. Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series III. Writings of Others and Series V. Personal and Professional Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents Hollo's personal and professional relationships with other poets, writers, and artists, including John Ashbery, Amiri Baraka (LeRoi Jones), Ted Berrigan, Rachel Blau DuPlessis, Diane Di Prima, Allen Ginsberg, Robert Grenier, Bobbie Louise Hawkins, Langston Hughes, Gerrit Lansing, Gerard Malanga, Ron Padgett, John Taggart, and Arthur Sze. Other correspondents include fellow Finnish writers Gösta Ågren, Paavo Haavikko, Sinikka Laine, and Pentti Saarikoski. Subject files contain correspondence organized by topic, including awards, book reviews, publishing, professional inquiries, teaching, and travel.

did.unittitle: Bridwell, Tom and Kitchell, Marilyn and Samara
Di Prima, Diane and Powell, Sheppard
Friedman, Ben, Richard, Darlene, Peter, and Art
Hornick, Lita Kulchur and Nasdor, Marc
Kalmus, Baker, Harding-Morick, Ainsworth, and Price, Prosodia
McGuffie, Jessie and Hamilton Finlay, Ian
Stanford, David, Slokak, Paul, and Richards, Tony
American-Scandinavian Foundation prize, Haavikko's selected poems
Bios and reviews by Anselm, recommendations
Corrections to published work (letter to editors)
Finnish (and a bit of German and Swedish)
Finnish Center for the International Theater Institute
Letters to press and publishers from Hollo
Miscellaneous correspondence, replies, book orders, applications
Present state of West is Left on the Map
Travel, Lufthansa ticket reimbursement due to heart surgery
University of Iowa International Writing Program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drafts, notes, typescripts, manuscripts, and other materials related to works translated, written, or edited by Hollo. Included are Hollo's translations of poetry by Sonja Åkesson, Allen Ginsberg, Maria Ranier Gerhardt, Pavo Haavikko, Paul Klee, Pentti Saarikoski, Dylan Thomas, Matti Tiisala, and William Carlos Williams into English, French, Finnish, German, and Swedish. Original works by Hollo consist of poetry, articles, essays, and short stories. Works edited by Hollo include drafts of poetry and books by writers such as Joe Cardarelli, Joseph Gugliemi, and Piero Heligzer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Translations, Original Works, and Edited Works. Arranged alphabetically by folder title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series V. Personal and Professional Papers.

did.unittitle: "En vieläkään osaa elää"/"I Don't Know How to Live"

"Marriage" by Gregory Corso and "The Death of Van Gogh's Ear"

"Perimmaisten Kysymysten Äärellä" ("Fundamental Questions Overlooking the Water")

Thomas, Dylan, Maitometsässä ( Under Milk Wood )

Drafts and notes [annotated poetry typescripts, general]

Handwritten notes in German and Finnish

"On Stars, Trees, Two Annes, and for the One Who's Here"

title.part: Pick Up the House: Poems by Anselm Hollo

Salt Lake City, proposal for 100 poems
"So That Friends May Enter" essay on Darrell Gray
"Some Thoughts on Ted Berrigan and His Work"
"Vikings" from The Legacy of the Vikings symposium
Work by A.H., texts in English, Finnish, German
Works by A.H., texts in German and Finnish

From The Maine Book , includes letters from Hollo
Gugliemi, Joseph, K, Or the Tale of Passage
Heligzer, Piero, A Purchase in the White Botanica

title.part: Hulk's Green Kid: An Alphabet and Numbers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains works by other writers, including Mary Abrams, Ted Berrigan, Robert Grenier, Kathleen McGill, Charles Olson, Jane Wodening, and Laura Wright, as well as their related correspondence with Hollo.

did.unittitle: Arguelles, Ivan and Bennett, John M.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains administrative files, course outlines, ungraded student works such as poetry, essays, and translations, and other materials for courses taught by Hollo at the Naropa Institute and the University of Colorado at Boulder.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Course Materials, and Student Works.

did.unittitle: SWP 2009 How to Say What You Did Not Mean to Say

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains conference materials, grant applications, awards, reviews of Hollo's works, travel logs, a small amount of correspondence, clippings, greeting cards, ticket stubs, and assorted memorabilia. Also included are some writings by Hollo, notebooks, composition books, address books, personal journals, and materials from Hollo's memorial service.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: General, Notebooks, Photographs, and Ephemera.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional printed materials are located in Series VII. Printed Materials.

did.unittitle: "Add-verse" [poetry project by Allan and Gloria Graham]
"The Child's Guide to Knowledge" [poem by Ezra Pound]
Color photocopies, front cover, Buffalo Bill stories

Presidential directive on safeguarding national security information

Stuff to be commented upon [poetry from Matt Hohner]

Various [articles, notes, poems, translations by Hollo]
Xerox [copies of news clippings by Hollo]
Hollo and Hollo, Tamsin in Lynchburg, Virginia at Sweetbriar College
[French line eastbound passage ticket and postcard]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains collages, sketches, and drawings by Hollo; collaborations between Hollo and his wife, Jane Dalrymple-Hollo; and artwork by others, including R.H. Hurst, Linda Murray, and George Schneeman.

did.unittitle: "For Anselm and Josephine, from R.H. Hurst" [color drawing]
[Portraits of family by Alice Codrescu, watercolor]
"The New Censorship: The Monthly Journal of the Next Savage State," Anselm Hollo and Jane Dalrymple-Hollo [drawings]
"For Jane, A Spider at the Pyramid Missed You!," Frederick
"These are Mammals Just Coming out of Water to Air," George Schneeman [oil pastel]
The Books of Hollo: A Bibliographic Appreciation Compiled and Edited by Lee Ballentine and Laura Wright [storyboard]
"Mandrake and I: A Christmas Card for Janey"
[Collages by Anselm Hollo and Jane Dalrymple-Hollo]
[Drawing by Ambrose Hollo and Jane Dalrymple-Hollo]
[ The South Side Monitor pressed print]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes books, serial publications, brochures, pamphlets, broadsides, and newspapers pertaining to or featuring Hollo's writing. Also included are newspapers containing articles in memoriam of Allen Ginsberg.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Books, Serial Publications, Brochures and Pamphlets, Broadsides, and Newspapers. Arranged chronologically by publication date.

title.part: Buffalo Bill and the Lost Indian; or, Pawnee Bill Guesses Right

Buffalo Bill and the Border Belle; or, Pawnee Bill's Search for a Lost Heir
Buffalo Bill and the Lost Trail; or, Pawnee Bill's Great Chase
Buffalo Bill's Clever Capture; or, Pawnee Bill Among the Sioux

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer and audiovisual media including compact discs, floppy disks, videocassettes, audiocassettes, and film reels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in two subseries by format.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 34 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 35 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
